articles.summary: Even with AI, product teams often take months to deliver. Why? Because tool switching, slow builds, and coordination overhead haven't gone away.

articles.title: 5 WAYS PRODUCT DISCOVERY BREAKS DOWN
articles.summary: Product discovery breaks down when companies fast-track “must-have” features and lack clear outcome-based goals. Evidence-guided discovery with shared metrics and measurable outcomes helps teams prioritize better ideas and avoid feature-factory behavior.

articles.title: WTF DOES A PRODUCT MANAGER DO? (AND WHY ENGINEERS SHOULD CARE)
articles.summary: As AI reduces the cost of building software, the real challenge becomes deciding what to build, pushing engineers to adopt product management skills. The three most important are gathering context, tracking results through feedback loops, and communicating decisions in a way that drives action.

articles.title: DANCING WITH PROBLEMS
articles.summary: Product problems rarely have a single clear definition. Teams must continually move between behavioral, contextual, and strategic perspectives to understand them. Product leaders create shared understanding by helping teams explore these layers together and refine the real problem over time.

articles.title: WILL FEATURES EVEN EXIST? HOW AI IS FORCING SAAS TO RETHINK THE PRODUCT ITSELF
articles.summary: AI is eroding the traditional SaaS model built around predefined features. The real moat will shift to platforms that let customers generate and govern their own workflows securely and reliably.

articles.title: FORRESTER + HARVARD BUSINESS REVIEW REPORT: REIMAGINING PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT WITH AI
articles.summary: AI has launched product development into uncharted terrain. While many are thriving in this turbulence, others still struggle to unlock AI's full potential. Miro commissioned Forrester Consulting and Harvard Business Review to conduct a widescale study of global product leaders [9] -- covering potential wins, current roadblocks, and

articles.title: HOW GOOD IS YOUR BACKLOG? HOW TO SHAPE THE WORK FOR PRODUCT SUCCESS
articles.summary: When backlog items feel too big or vague, the problem is usually poor shaping around customer needs, not just missing detail. Teams should shape work together through problem clarification, workshops, and spikes so they can learn faster, avoid handoff friction, and deliver value in smaller, clearer steps.

articles.title: KILL YOUR DARLINGS (22 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: Flatlining products that generate some revenue but show no growth often block better opportunities. Product leaders must deliberately sunset them as portfolio decisions to free teams to discover the next source of growth.

articles.title: VISHAL KAPOOR'S 10 RULES FOR BUILDING HONEST PRODUCTS WITH AI
articles.summary: AI can accelerate experimentation and prototyping, but building trustworthy products still requires human judgment, customer understanding, and first-principles thinking. Teams should use AI to explore ideas faster while prioritizing simplicity, transparency, and real customer value.

articles.title: WHAT EXCELLENT GROWTH TEAMS SEE THAT OTHERS MISS (43 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Great growth teams don't chase experiments or tactics. They identify and remove the real friction blocking users from getting value. Sustainable growth comes from deep customer understanding, faster learning, and compounding the insights that truly matter.

articles.title: TRANSLATING
articles.summary: Product leaders should translate product metrics into clear financial impact when speaking with executives.

articles.title: FINDING THE ROLE OF HUMANS IN AI PRODUCTS
articles.summary: As AI agents become increasingly autonomous, the human role shifts from doing tasks to supervising systems that do them.
